column2: # Build community matrix: each row = one transect, columns = 5 broad cover categories.
# Using proportions (not raw counts) so transects are comparable regardless of
# PERMANOVA (adonis2) — tests whether the multivariate community composition
# differs significantly between reef sites. Uses Bray-Curtis dissimilarity,
# which handles proportional data well and down-weights rare categories.

Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
Permutation: free
Number of permutations: 999
adonis2(formula = comm_matrix ~ dive_site, data = substrate_clean, permutations = 999, method = "bray")
# PERMDISP — checks a key assumption of PERMANOVA: that within-group dispersions
# (spread around group centroids) are homogeneous across sites. If p < 0.05 here,
# any PERMANOVA significance may reflect differences in dispersion rather than
Permutation test for homogeneity of multivariate dispersions
